primary_sources.title: Node.js File system documentation
primary_sources.relevance: Documents exclusive-create file opening and rename primitives used for lock acquisition and replacement writes.

finding: A one-time acceptance decision must be persisted behind an exclusive lock and bounded retention policy; an in-memory seen-ID array cannot prevent duplicate acceptance across reloads or competing processes.
uncertainty: rename durability depends on filesystem and directory-sync behavior
lock files can remain after process termination
the implementation coordinates processes sharing one filesystem only
retention duration is policy, not a universal replay-safety guarantee
falsification_route: kill the process between temporary write and rename and inspect recovery behavior
kill the process while holding the lock and verify stale-lock handling
run consumers across distinct hosts or storage mounts and demonstrate duplicate acceptance
exceed retention and capacity bounds and verify documented replay window
